backlog.title: Verification & Documentation Coherence Gate (Delta v006)
backlog.description: This is a VERIFICATION + DOC-AUDIT delta, NOT greenfield implementation. The #@file extension is already fully implemented (file-injector.ts, 1114 lines) with 182 tests passing green and tsc --strict clean. Three requirement-level changes were introduced in PRD.md since session-005 (4-source config merge; depth-uniform markdown resolution with no cwd fallback; depth-uniform bare-@ matching) — all three are ALREADY implemented and tested green. The legitimate work is: (1) a confirmation gate that the green state holds against the updated spec, and (2) closing one known stale-doc nit (PRD.md Done-definition says '24' but §11 now lists 32 test cases). No new code is expected unless the gate surfaces a genuine regression.

milestones.title: Confirm v006 delta requirements + close stale-doc nit
milestones.description: Confirm the current repo satisfies the three delta requirement changes (4-source config, depth-uniform resolution, depth-uniform bare-@) via the existing test suites + typecheck, then close the one stale-doc nit in PRD.md's Done-definition.

tasks.title: Verification gate — confirm repo satisfies the three §1 delta requirements
tasks.description: Run the full verification suite (three test files + tsc --strict) and confirm the repo satisfies the three delta PRD §1 requirement changes. All three are already implemented and green per prior research — this task records the gated confirmation. If any check is RED, the only in-scope code work is fixing the offending function without weakening strictness or deleting tests (treat a red gate as a regression to repair, not a redesign).

subtasks.title: Run all three test suites — confirm 182 passed, 0 failed
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Prior research (plan/006_1862a6537500/architecture/system_context.md) confirmed all 182 tests pass: file-injector.test.mjs (122), relative-imports.test.mjs (38), import-behavior.test.mjs (22). This subtask is a confirmation gate, not new implementation.
2. INPUT: The existing test files at repo root: file-injector.test.mjs, relative-imports.test.mjs, import-behavior.test.mjs. These are zero-dep Node scripts that load the real extension via Pi's jiti loader.
3. LOGIC: Run each suite in sequence: `node ./file-injector.test.mjs && node ./relative-imports.test.mjs && node ./import-behavior.test.mjs`. Assert each prints 'X passed, 0 failed' where X totals 182. If ANY suite fails, do NOT weaken tests or delete cases — diagnose the failing case, identify the offending function in file-injector.ts, and apply the minimal fix that makes the requirement pass. The three §1 requirements being confirmed: (A) 4-source config merge with trust gate, (B) depth-uniform markdown resolution (dirname(abs), no cwd fallback), (C) depth-uniform bare-@ matching. Do NOT mock anything — these are integration tests against the real extension.
4. OUTPUT: Record the pass/fail count for each suite. If all green, mark complete. If any red, fix the code and re-run until green.
5. DOCS: none — this is a verification subtask with no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Run tsc --strict typecheck — confirm 0 errors
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Prior research (plan/006_1862a6537500/architecture/external_deps.md) validated all Pi API imports against v0.80.8 — resizeImage, formatDimensionNote, CONFIG_DIR_NAME, getAgentDir, ResizedImage, ExtensionAPI, ImageContent all confirmed exported. typecheck was clean (0 errors) as of research time.
2. INPUT: file-injector.ts (1114 lines), tsconfig.json, scripts/typecheck.mjs. The typecheck script runs tsc --strict against the extension file.
3. LOGIC: Run `node ./scripts/typecheck.mjs`. Assert output contains '0 errors'. If errors appear, diagnose each — it would indicate either a type regression in file-injector.ts or a breaking API change in the Pi package. Fix the offending code (do not suppress with @ts-ignore or loosen strictness). The installed package is at /home/dustin/.local/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/ (v0.80.8).
4. OUTPUT: Record the typecheck result. If clean, mark complete. If errors, fix and re-run.
5. DOCS: none — verification subtask, no surface change.

subtasks.title: Confirm the three §1 requirements are specifically exercised in the test suites
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The delta PRD §2 maps specific test cases to each requirement: (A) 4-source config → file-injector.test.mjs cases T2.S1-e/-f/-g; (B) depth-uniform/no-cwd-fallback → relative-imports.test.mjs cases A1/A7/A9/C1/C2; (C) depth-uniform bare-@ → relative-imports.test.mjs + import-behavior.test.mjs. This subtask verifies those specific cases exist, run, and assert the right behavior.
2. INPUT: Green test suites from P1.M1.T1.S1 and clean typecheck from P1.M1.T1.S2. The three test files and file-injector.ts.
3. LOGIC: For each of the three §1 requirements, locate the named test cases in the test files and confirm: (A) config cases assert settings.json fileInjector key enables the option (e), trust gate applies to the key (f), dedicated file overrides settings.json key within scope (g); (B) relative cases assert baseDir-wins-over-cwd (A1), missing-in-baseDir→null-no-fallback (A7), resolution-independent-of-process.cwd (A9), deep-nested scenarios (C1/C2); (C) bare-@ cases assert honoring at first imported file and across a chain, plus inert when off at every depth. If any named case is missing or asserts the wrong thing, add or fix it (do not weaken assertions).
4. OUTPUT: A confirmation table mapping each §1 requirement → the test cases that exercise it → pass/fail. If all present and green, mark complete.
5. DOCS: none — verification subtask, no surface change.

tasks.title: Doc audit — fix stale test-case count + confirm documentation coherence
tasks.description: This IS the changeset-level documentation sweep for the delta (Mode B per SOW §5). (1) Fix the one known stale-doc nit: PRD.md Done-definition says 'all 24 manual test cases' but §11 now lists 32. (2) Confirm README.md and JSDoc are coherent with the shipped behavior — no edits expected unless genuine drift is found (prior research found none).

subtasks.title: Fix PRD.md Done-definition: 'all 24' → 'all 32' test cases
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Prior research (plan/006_1862a6537500/architecture/research-doc-audit.md) confirmed: PRD.md:1189 (Appendix A, Done-definition) says 'all 24 manual test cases' but §11's manual test matrix now lists 32 rows (#1–#32, no gaps). This is the ONLY stale test-count reference in the file. The '24' at PRD.md:971 is row #24 (a row index, not a count) — correct, do not touch.
2. INPUT: PRD.md at line 1189. The exact current text is: '**Done-definition:** all **24** manual test cases in §11 pass; ...'
3. LOGIC: In PRD.md, change 'all **24** manual test cases' to 'all **32** manual test cases' at the Done-definition line. This is a one-token fix. Verify the §11 matrix does indeed have 32 rows (grep for '| [0-9]' in the manual test matrix block). Do NOT modify any other part of PRD.md.
4. OUTPUT: PRD.md with the corrected count. Confirm no other stale '24' test-count references exist (grep -n '24.*test\|24.*case\|all 24' PRD.md should return nothing after the fix).
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This subtask IS the changeset-level doc fix — the PRD's own Done-definition is the summary doc that drifted. This rides with the doc-audit task, not with implementing subtasks (there are none to implement — this is a verification delta).

subtasks.title: Confirm README.md markdownBareAtImports config section coherence with §4.6
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Prior research (plan/006_1862a6537500/architecture/research-doc-audit.md) confirmed README.md:88–117 documents BOTH config forms (dedicated file + settings.json fileInjector key), all 4 precedence locations, the trust gate, and depth-uniform resolution. No drift was found. This subtask re-confirms that coherence still holds.
2. INPUT: README.md (134 lines), specifically the '### Optional: bare-@ markdown imports' section (lines 88–117). PRD.md §4.6 (the authoritative spec for config sources and precedence).
3. LOGIC: Read README.md lines 88–117. Verify against PRD §4.6: (a) both config forms documented with examples; (b) four precedence locations listed in order; (c) trust gate ('trusted project only') stated for project sources; (d) depth-uniform property stated. If all coherent (expected), no edit needed — mark complete. If any claim drifted from the shipped code, correct the doc to match code behavior (not the other way).
4. OUTPUT: Confirmation that README.md config docs are coherent with §4.6, or the minimal edit if drift is found.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] If this subtask finds drift in README.md, the fix rides here with this subtask. If no drift (expected), DOCS line is 'none — no user-facing/config/API surface change'.

subtasks.title: Confirm readConfig/SETTINGS_KEY/injectMarkdown JSDoc coherence with §4.5/§4.6
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Prior research (plan/006_1862a6537500/architecture/research-config-and-resolution.md) confirmed the JSDoc on readConfig (file-injector.ts:167–183) documents all 4 sources, precedence, trust gate, and SETTINGS_KEY form. The injectMarkdown JSDoc documents dirname(abs) resolution and bareAt threading. No drift was found.

3. LOGIC: Read the JSDoc on readConfig and injectMarkdown in file-injector.ts. Verify: (a) readConfig JSDoc lists all 4 sources in precedence order with trust gate + SETTINGS_KEY form; (b) SETTINGS_KEY const comment explains the settings.json key; (c) injectMarkdown JSDoc notes dirname(abs) resolution (depth-uniform, no cwd fallback) and bareAt threading. If all coherent (expected), no edit needed. If drift found, correct the JSDoc to match shipped behavior.
4. OUTPUT: Confirmation that JSDoc is coherent with §4.5/§4.6, or the minimal fix if drift is found.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] If this subtask finds JSDoc drift, the fix rides here. If no drift (expected), DOCS line is 'none — no user-facing/config/API surface change'.
